title: Resilience Routine Checklist
description: Build and track your daily resilience routine. These evidence-based habits form the foundation for long-term anxiety management.
instructions: Review each resilience-building habit and check the ones you are consistently practicing. Aim to add one new habit per week. Revisit this checklist regularly to maintain accountability.

items.label: I have a consistent morning routine that starts my day calmly
I maintain a regular sleep schedule (7-9 hours, consistent times)
I eat regular, balanced meals (not skipping meals)
I get at least 30 minutes of physical activity most days
I limit caffeine and avoid it after early afternoon
I limit alcohol consumption (which can worsen anxiety)
I stay well-hydrated throughout the day
I practice mindfulness or meditation for at least 5-10 minutes daily
I use breathing techniques when I notice early signs of anxiety
I notice and challenge anxious thoughts rather than accepting them as facts
I continue to face feared situations rather than avoiding them
I maintain regular social connections (even small ones)
I maintain healthy boundaries in relationships
I practice self-compassion when I have setbacks
I monitor my warning signs and take early action when anxiety increases
I know when to seek professional support and have a plan for doing so

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational purposes only. If your anxiety is significantly affecting your daily life, please consider professional support. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
